# ADR 0001: Extend contentful/core BaseClient Instead of Implementing HTTP from Scratch

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Contentful maintains multiple PHP SDKs (CMA and CDA). Both need: auth header injection, a consistent SDK user-agent, rate-limit handling, response deserialization infrastructure (`ResourceArray`, `Link`, `BaseResourceBuilder`), and PSR-compliant HTTP abstractions over Guzzle.

Duplicating this logic in each SDK would create maintenance drift — bugs fixed in one would need to be manually ported to the other.

## Decision

The CMA SDK depends on `contentful/core` (`^4.0`) and extends `BaseClient` / `BaseResourceBuilder`. HTTP dispatch, auth headers, user-agent strings, and the `ResourceArray`/`Link` types all live in `contentful/core`. This SDK owns only the CMA-specific resources, mappers, and URI configuration.

Evidence: `src/Client.php` extends `Contentful\Core\Api\BaseClient`; `src/ResourceBuilder.php` extends `Contentful\Core\ResourceBuilder\BaseResourceBuilder`.

## Consequences

- Consistent behavior between CDA and CMA PHP SDKs at the HTTP layer.
- Core changes (e.g. Guzzle version bumps, retry logic) are applied once and inherited.
- This SDK has a hard runtime dependency on `contentful/core` — a major core version bump is a breaking change here too.
- Contributors working on HTTP or auth behavior must work in `contentful/contentful-core.php`, not here.
